response:
**Results**

We evaluated ten experimental variants of our proposed method on the validation set, each averaged over five random seeds. Our primary baseline, which omits the proposed component, achieves a mean accuracy of 67.8% (±0.6). Among all variants incorporating our approach, the majority yield validation accuracies in the range of 64.7% to 67.2%, with standard deviations between 0.7 and 1.0, comparable to the baseline in terms of stability.

Critically, one configuration—variant_08—substantially outperforms the baseline, achieving a validation accuracy of 70.4% (±0.5). This represents a significant absolute improvement of +2.6 percentage points over the baseline, setting a new best for this task. Notably, variant_08 is also the most consistent, exhibiting the lowest standard deviation across seeds, further underscoring the robustness of the improvement.

The results suggest that while the majority of design choices explored do not surpass the strong baseline, the correct instantiation of our proposed method can yield marked performance gains. The superior performance and consistency of variant_08 validate the effectiveness of our approach when appropriately configured. This indicates that the proposed component, when integrated with the optimal hyperparameter and architectural settings, addresses key limitations of the baseline and leads to state-of-the-art results on this benchmark.

In summary, these findings demonstrate that our method is capable of delivering substantial accuracy improvements, provided careful selection of the accompanying design choices. We provide an analysis of the ablation variants and further discussion of the successful configuration in the following sections.
